title: Epidendrum aristotelea
taxonomy: Epidendrum aristotelea belongs to the family Orchidaceae, which is commonly known as the orchid family. This classification places it within the order Asparagales, a large group of flowering plants that also includes asparagus and lilies. At the genus level, Epidendrum aristotelea is classified as a member of the genus Epidendrum, which comprises around 600 species of orchids. The species is further divided into various subcategories, including the subfamily Epidendroideae and the tribe Maxillarieae. This classification highlights the plant's unique characteristics and its relationship to other orchid species.
uses: Epidendrum aristotelea is native to tropical regions and its medicinal properties have been explored.
